hypothesis: If a closed-loop Bayesian optimization platform is used to iteratively tune bicarbonate temperature, concentration and flow, KOH concentration and flow, and applied current density in a bipolar-membrane reactive carbon electrolyzer with Ag nanoparticle gas-diffusion electrodes, then reactor yield will converge to a higher optimum in substantially fewer experiments than uninformed exploration because Gaussian process–guided acquisition efficiently learns and exploits nonlinear parameter interactions and importance.
